failedAssertions.reason: CLI task command fails due to daemon socket reading bug
CLI list command fails due to daemon socket reading bug
CLI watch command fails due to daemon socket reading bug
CLI approve command fails due to daemon socket reading bug
Unix socket communication fundamentally broken due to buffer initialization bug
Error handling returns wrong message due to socket reading failure
End-to-end task execution blocked by socket communication failure

appliedUpdates.description: Added daemon socket reading bug to known issues - vec![0u8; 4096] pre-initializes buffer causing tokio read_buf to read 0 bytes

rootCause.description: Buffer initialized with `vec![0u8; 4096]` creates a Vec with 4096 bytes already used. Tokio's read_buf() appends to buffer but cannot because length==capacity (buffer appears full). Daemon reads 0 bytes and fails to parse empty string as JSON.
rootCause.fix: Change `let mut buf = vec![0u8; 4096];` to `let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(4096);` and then the first read_buf will properly append data.
